content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Charles P. Scully 
300 Montgomery Street 
Suite 735 
San Francisco, CA 
94104-1909 
Dear Mr. Scully: 
July 5, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-348 
We have received your letter of June 5, 1989, seeking written 
confirmation of the advice I provided to you in our telephone 
conversation of June 2, 1989, regarding your status as a lobbyist 
under the Political Reform Act. 1 
This is to confirm that your 
letter accurately reflects that advice. 
However, I would like to 
clarify a couple of points made in your letter. 
You need not register as a lobbyist as long as (1) you have 
less than 25 contacts with legislative or agency officials in any 
two consecutive months and (2) you, as an individual, receive less 
than $2,000 in a calendar month for your lobbying activities, 
including any preparatory work you may do in order to testify 
before a legislative committee. 
Therefore, regardless of the fact 
that you are a "salaried employee" of the law firm, you must 
calculate the portion of your time that is consumed by lobbying 
activities on a monthly basis in order to determine whether you 
receive $2,000 in a calendar month for lobbying activities. 
A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience. 
If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
E . 
By: 
. 
::~~~ 
vi 
B aaten-Moen 
p~tical Reform Consultant 
Enclosures 
I 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322,5660 


LAW OFFICES 
OF 
CHARLES P. SCULLY, INC. 
300 MONTGOMERV STREET 
SUITE 735 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 1l4104-11109 
'89 
CHARLES P. SCULLV (1915-\ 985) 
DONALD C. CARROLL 
June 5, 1989 
TELEPHONE 
362-0241 
CHARLES P. SCULLV,II 
AREA CODE 415 
~44 
Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Mr. Bratten-Moen: 
This will simply serve to confirm our 
telephone conversation of June 2, 1989. 
As I explained 
these offices are registered as a "lobbying firm" and 
Mr. Donald Carroll is registered as a lobbyist. 
The principal activity requiring registration 
as a lobbying firm is the fact that these offices draft 
legislation for clients who in turn seek an author and 
support passage of many particular items of legislation. 
We have previously been advised that in terms 
of the drafting process there is no need for me to 
register separately as a lobbyist given the firm's 
registration. 
We recently represented a client who was 
involved in negotiations for a package of reforms 
in a particular legislative area. 
That client has 
requested that I testify at various committee and sub-
committee hearings in order to explain various proposed 
sections from a ~egal perspective. 
I am not involved in 
any lobbying activity other than those described above, 
i.e. I am not being requested to discuss the proposed 
legislation in a one-and-one setting with any particular 
legislator. 
From our discussion it is my understanding that 
I need not register personally as a lobbyist unless I 
appear as a witness before committees 25 or more times 
during any two month period. 
Of course any compensation 
these offices receive due to my services to the client 
will be reported on our offices' lobbyist report forms. 
Our office is structured as a corporation and, accordingly, 
I am a salaried employee shareholder in the same. 


Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Page Two 
June 5, 1989 
Should I be mistaken in terms of my understanding 
of obligations pursuant to the Act related to the afore-
said activities, I would appreciate your contacting me 
by the means most convenient to you in order to assure 
that I personally, and these offices as an entity, fully 
comply with all requirements of the Act. 
Thank you. 
CPSII:bjs 
OPE-3-AFL-CIO 
cc: Donald C. Carroll 
Very truly yours, 
LAW OFFICES OF 
CHARLES P. SCULLY, INC.

Dear Mr. scully: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on June 6, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or

sections.question: Does Charles P. Scully II need to register personally as a lobbyist when testifying before legislative committees if his law firm is already registered as a lobbying firm, and he is a salaried employee of the firm?
sections.conclusion: You need not register as a lobbyist as long as (1) you have less than 25 contacts with legislative or agency officials in any two consecutive months and (2) you, as an individual, receive less than $2,000 in a calendar month for your lobbying activities, including any preparatory work you may do in order to testify before a legislative committee. Therefore, regardless of the fact that you are a "salaried employee" of the law firm, you must calculate the portion of your time that is consumed by lobbying activities on a monthly basis in order to determine whether you receive $2,000 in a calendar month for lobbying activities.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a salaried attorney at a registered lobbying firm must register personally as a lobbyist when testifying before legislative committees on behalf of a client, given that the firm already reports his compensation and activities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that personal lobbyist registration is not required if the requester has fewer than 25 contacts with legislative/agency officials in any two consecutive months AND receives less than $2,000 per calendar month for lobbying activities. The FPPC clarified that despite salaried employment, the attorney must calculate the portion of compensation attributable to lobbying work monthly to determine if the $2,000 threshold is exceeded.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated June 5, 1989) and the FPPC response letter (dated July 5, 1989) confirming prior telephone advice with clarifications. Also includes an administrative receipt letter from June 8, 1989. The key advice is contained in the July 5, 1989 response from B. Braaten-Moen.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Charles P. Scully II need to register personally as a lobbyist when testifying before legislative committees if his law firm is already registered as a lobbying firm, and he is a salaried employee of the firm?

CONCLUSION: You need not register as a lobbyist as long as (1) you have less than 25 contacts with legislative or agency officials in any two consecutive months and (2) you, as an individual, receive less than $2,000 in a calendar month for your lobbying activities, including any preparatory work you may do in order to testify before a legislative committee. Therefore, regardless of the fact that you are a "salaried employee" of the law firm, you must calculate the portion of your time that is consumed by lobbying activities on a monthly basis in order to determine whether you receive $2,000 in a calendar month for lobbying activities.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Charles P. Scully II's request confirming prior telephone advice regarding lobbyist registration requirements for a salaried attorney at a registered lobbying firm who testifies before legislative committees. The FPPC clarified the dual threshold test for avoiding personal registration: fewer than 25 legislative/agency contacts in any two consecutive months and less than $2,000 monthly compensation for lobbying activities.
